findings.message: D2 lists direct inner attributes like `#![doc(hidden)]` as required detections, but then says the judgment target is any `syn item`. A crate/file-level inner attribute lives on `syn::File::attrs`, not on an item, so an implementation that follows the item-only wording can miss the exact inner-attribute form the ADR says is in scope. The plan should explicitly include file-level attrs as scan targets, or narrow the inner-attribute claim.

The same sentence says doc comment string literals do not appear as attributes in syn, but Rust doc comments are represented as `#[doc = "..."]` attributes. The intended behavior can still exclude them, but the ADR should describe that as ignoring `doc` name-value attributes and only matching `doc(hidden)` list forms, not as relying on doc comments being absent from the AST.
